overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto has a strong social media presence with a total follower count of 3580 across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky. His content focuses primarily on research related to computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn showing higher like and comment counts.

contentThemes.description: Posts about research projects, findings, and methodologies related to computational social science.
Content focused on the detection, analysis, and mitigation of disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior online.
Posts related to hiring, grant awards, and team achievements.

recommendations: Tailor content to each platform's audience. For example, share professional updates and job opportunities on LinkedIn, and research snippets on Bluesky and Mastodon.
Increase engagement by asking direct questions and encouraging discussion within posts.
Use relevant hashtags to broaden the reach of posts, particularly on Bluesky and Mastodon.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits moderate engagement with good repost numbers. Fabio can potentially increase likes and replies by initiating more conversations.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon posts have decent likes and reposts, but are lower than other platforms. Consider using Mastodon to share more in-depth insights or engage in topical discussions with the AOIR community.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn posts receive the highest engagement in terms of likes and comments, likely due to the professional nature of the platform. Continue sharing professional achievements, job opportunities, and industry-related news.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
